ogTitle: Cessnock vs Chain Valley Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cessnock and Chain Valley Bay.
heading: Cessnock vs Chain Valley Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cessnock, New South Wales and Chain Valley Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cessnock has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Chain Valley Bay (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cessnock or Chain Valley Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cessnock has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Chain Valley Bay scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cessnock scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Chain Valley Bay scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cessnock or Chain Valley Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cessnock has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
